What's going on guys? So I just want to show you the follow up to the thing that I was just showing you guys with the clipboard system. So simply put, you're going to have multiple clipboards. Some of them are going to be hanging on hooks and some of them are going to be on your desk. So the first thing is going to be a sign in sheet, right? That's going to be on a clipboard and that is going to be wherever your lobby is, etc. Okay? So that's going to be not on a clipboard but not on a hook. So that I'll just be sitting around. Then you're going to have your salesman, which may be you, which will have the way in sheet. So this is going to be scheduling for the nutrition orientation. Okay? And there's a reason you'll see why I'm putting it like this in a moment. So you'll have your second one here. This again is going to be right where you're selling on your sales table. Okay? Now, after this point, after the way in is when you're going to have your, the weekly way in sheet. So that's we're going to fill out with week zero, which will look like this. And I have week one, week two, week three, etc. And then week five. Okay? So I'm going to show you like this, how this works. So every single week, what you're going to do is you're going to move the clipboard from hook to hook on the wall. I know this sounds ridiculously simple, but this is the simplest way that this can work. So, after they've completed the first week, you move week zero to week one, and then they're going to fill out the second column for week one, and then they'll fill out the third column for week two, and they'll fill out the fourth column for week three, etc. And the nice thing is that when you have your abandoned hope closed, it's just going to be a week three. You can reach out to those people and then with your week three reach out, you can reach out with that intention of saying, hey, you know, when's a good time for us to, for you to meet in, or can you stay after class, etc. Okay? I like doing abandoned hopes on Wednesdays, so I would always book my Wednesdays or clear them so that I could always do abandoned hopes on Wednesday nights. Okay? So you can choose to do that or not. So I'm going to say abandoned hope goes right here. And as a quick reminder for you, you need to be reaching out during each of these weeks prior because if you all of a sudden reach out halfway through to try and talk to them, they're going to know that it's a sales meeting and you don't want that. But if you've been talking in this whole time, then it'll seem natural and they'll be totally cool with it. And that meeting should be an accountability meeting to see if you make any adjustments and you should provide value in that, which I'll get to more in the resell portion of the training. Now, once you're at week five, this one isn't going to go into a hook for week six. Okay? So instead, what you're going to have here is the way out. Okay? So you're going to have the way out, which is essentially the same sheet as this guy. So the salesman and the way out sheet are almost identical, except this is where you're going to be scheduling the times for them to weigh out. I like 15, I did it in 15 minutes. I think I said 15 minutes in the video. If you really, if this is your first time, I would say give yourself 20 minutes for each of these consultations and these should be one on one. And if you're like, man, that sounds like a lot of consults. If I were to tell you, you're going to be meeting with 10 people who all are pre-qualified and you have their credit card information to sign them up for a membership. You would do that and all of them are going to give you a testimonial. So don't be lazy. Meet with them one-on-one. They give you a bunch of money and like you need to celebrate the victory with them and explain what the next steps are. Okay? So all in all here, you're going to have this one, this sign-in sheet right here, which is going to be in your lobby. Your salesman is going to have this on the sales desk. And then after you have your nutrition orientation, which you will fill this out for each of your clients, you're going to reach out to them every single week and every week, you're going to move the clipboard from one to the next to the next. Okay? At week three, you have your abandoned hope meeting. And then at week five, instead of moving the clipboard over, they are then going to schedule themselves for that way out. And then you can obviously double check with them and follow up on week five to make sure that they have scheduled their way out so that you can get their pictures so that you can explain to them what the next steps are. All right? So this is the world's simplest system. I had a $30,000 system with tags and all sorts of stuff. And this is the easiest way that we've been able to manage it. My trainer's liked it too. So I hope you enjoy it and I hope it makes sense to you. It is very straightforward and unmess-uppable.
